aah: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Abu'.
acinar: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or located in an acinus.
aha: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ahanta.
aim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Aimol.
ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.
air: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Airoran.
airman: noun. A pilot of an aircraft.
ama: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Amanayé.
amah: noun. A woman employed to look after children; (formerly) a wet nurse.
amain: adverb. With all of one's might; mightily; forcefully, violently.
ami: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Amis.
amin: noun. An arbitrator who assessed and collected revenue in the pargana.
ana: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andaqui.
anarch: noun. The author of anarchy; one who excites revolt.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
anima: noun. The soul or animating principle of a living thing, especially as contrasted with the animus.
arc: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aramaic.
aria: noun. A musical piece written typically for a solo voice with orchestral accompaniment in an opera or cantata.
arm: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Armenian.
arnica: noun. Any of several plants, of the genus Arnica, considered to have medicinal properties, especially Arnica montana.
caiman: noun. Any of the relatively small crocodilians of the genus Caiman, within the family Alligatoridae.
cairn: noun. A rounded or conical heap of stones erected by early inhabitants of the British Isles, apparently as a sepulchral monument.
cam: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cèmuhî.
can: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chambri.
car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
carina: noun. A longitudinal ridge or projection like the keel of a boat.
carman: noun. A person who transported goods, usually with a horse and cart.
chain: noun. A series of interconnected rings or links usually made of metal.
chair: noun. An item of furniture used to sit on or in, comprising a seat, legs or wheels, back, and sometimes arm rests, for use by one person.
chairman: noun. A person presiding over a meeting, sometimes especially a man.
char: verb. To burn something to charcoal; to be burnt to charcoal.
charm: noun. An object, act or words believed to have magic power (usually carries a positive connotation).
chi: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Chinese.
chia: noun. A Mexican sage grown for its edible seeds, Salvia hispanica.
chin: noun. The bottom of a face, the typically jutting jawline below the mouth.
china: noun. Synonym of porcelain, a hard white translucent ceramic made from kaolin, now sometimes distinguished in reference to tableware as
chirm: noun. A din or confused noise, as of many voices, birdsong, etc.
cram: verb. To press, force, or drive, particularly in filling, or in thrusting one thing into another; to stuff; to fill to superfluity.
haar: noun. Thick, cold, wet fog along the northeastern coast of Northern England and Scotland.
hair: noun. A pigmented filament of keratin which grows from a follicle on the skin of humans and other mammals.
ham: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hewa.
hic: interjection. An approximation to the sound of a hiccup, used e.g. to indicate drunkenness.
him: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Himachali languages.
hin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Hindi.
ich: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Etkywan.
ihram: noun. The state of ritual purity and dedication of a Muslim hajj pilgrim to Mecca.
inarch: verb. To graft by uniting, as a scion, to a stock, without separating either from its root before the union is complete.
inch: noun. An English unit of length equal to 1/12 of a foot or 2.54 cm, conceived as roughly the width of a thumb.
maar: noun. A broad volcanic crater, usually filled with water to form a lake.
mac: symbol. ISO 639-2 /B language code for Macedonian.
main: adjective. Of chief or leading importance; prime, principal.
man: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mandingo.
mana: noun. Power, prestige; specifically, a form of supernatural energy in Polynesian religion that inheres in things or people.
mania: noun. Violent derangement of mind; madness; insanity.
maniac: noun. An insane person, especially one who suffers from a mania.
manic: adjective. Characterized by mania or craziness; wicked.
mar: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Marathi.
marc: noun. The refuse matter that remains after fruit, particularly grapes, has been pressed.
march: noun. A formal, rhythmic way of walking, used especially by soldiers, by bands, and in ceremonies.
marina: noun. A harbour for small boats.
mica: noun. Any of a group of hydrous aluminosilicate minerals characterized by highly perfect cleavage, so that they readily separate into very
mir: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isthmus Mixe.
nah: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Nahuan languages.
naira: noun. The official currency of Nigeria.
nam: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngan'gityemerri.
narc: noun. A police officer or federal agent assigned to or engaging in illegal narcotics control.
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
rah: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rabha.
raia: noun. Alternative form of raya (“type of historical territory”).
rain: noun. Condensed water falling from a cloud.
ram: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Canela.
ran: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Riantana.
ranch: noun. A large plot of land used for raising cattle, sheep or other livestock.
rani: noun. The wife of a rajah.
ria: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Reang.
rich: adjective. Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
rim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nyaturu.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
